purpose: Defines the `!track_resume [dashboardId]` command — lighter resume of a stalled !p_track swarm than !p_track_resume. Phase 1 reads core instructions (CLAUDE.md + tracker_master_instructions.md), resolves dashboard (auto-detects first resumable dashboard if not specified), reads init+all progress+logs+plan+project context, and CLASSIFIES every task into Completed/Failed/Stale In-Progress/Pending(ready)/Pending(blocked). For !track_resume purposes, treats ALL in_progress as stale (assumes previous master and its workers are dead). Phase 2 cleans stale progress files, builds full p_track-quality worker prompts with RESUME CONTEXT and PREVIOUS ATTEMPT sections, dispatches all ready. Phase 3 runs the standard execution loop with circuit breaker and final report.
gotchas: Treats ALL in_progress as stale — does NOT do the alive/uncertain/stale 3-way classification that !p_track_resume does (simpler but more aggressive)
Reads only 2 instruction files in Phase 1 (CLAUDE.md + tracker_master_instructions.md) vs !p_track_resume's 7 — lighter on instruction reading
If plan.json is missing, the master MUST regenerate it (redo deep-thinking pass) before re-dispatching — hooks block dispatch otherwise
Auto-detect logic: scan listDashboards() in order; first dashboard with non-null task and any incomplete/missing progress = resumable

Worker prompt template still uses the same p_track sections — TASK/DESCRIPTION/CONTEXT/PROJECT+TRACKER ROOT/CONVENTIONS/REFERENCE/UPSTREAM/CRITICAL/FILES/DIRECTORY/RESUME CONTEXT/PREVIOUS ATTEMPT (if retry)/PREPARATION/PROGRESS REPORTING/DEVIATION REPORTING/EXECUTION RULES/RETURN FORMAT
Final report is lighter than !p_track_resume — just summary table + log entry, no NON-NEGOTIABLE Resume Details section
Self-assessment 4-question gate is included verbatim in the worker prompt
patterns: Three phases: Phase 1 Assess Current State (Steps 1-7) → Phase 2 Dispatch (Steps 8-10) → Phase 3 Monitor+Standard Execution Loop (Steps 11-13)
5-row task classification (Completed, Failed, Stale In-Progress, Pending ready, Pending blocked)
Stale detection signals: no active worker process, started_at >15-min old with no recent milestones, master session restarted
Dispatch sequence: launch agent FIRST → log AFTER → one-line confirmation
Worker prompt RESUME CONTEXT sections: NOTE about resumed state → optional PREVIOUS ATTEMPT block (status/stage/summary/logs/deviations/ROOT CAUSE/REMEDIATION)
conventions: Top-of-file NON-NEGOTIABLE RULES (4 numbered rules — fewer than !p_track_resume's 7)
Quick Reference table comparing !track_resume vs !dispatch --ready vs !retry
Embedded full worker prompt template (same as !p_track Step 14 with RESUME CONTEXT additions)
